CWUR Dashboard Methodology

The Center for World University Rankings (CWUR) publishes the only global university ranking that measures the quality of education and training of students as well as the prestige of the faculty members and the quality of their research without relying on surveys and university data submissions.

1. The Four Main Pillars

Education (25%)

Measures quality of education based on the number of alumni who have won major international awards relative to the university's size.

Employability (25%)

Measures graduate career success based on the number of alumni holding top executive positions at the world's largest companies.

Faculty (10%)

Calculated by the number of faculty members who have won major international awards, prizes, and medals.

Research (40%)

The largest pillar, analyzing output, high-quality publications, influence, and citation counts for institutional researchers.

2. Key Takeaway

CWUR is known for being strictly data-driven and outcome-focused. It removes the "reputation bias" found in other rankings by using objective, verifiable metrics rather than academic surveys.
